basics.name: Alexander Nole
basics.label: Backend Developer | Java & .NET | Microservices | Full Stack Experience
basics.summary: Backend Developer with 1+ year of professional experience building APIs and distributed systems, and over 4 years of independent software development. Specialized in Java (Spring Boot) and .NET, with hands-on experience in microservices architecture, SaaS multitenant systems, and real-time communication. Additionally, experienced in integrating modern frontend technologies such as Next.js and Angular, enabling a strong understanding of end-to-end application flow.

work.name: Develtrex
work.summary: Contributed to a distributed microservices platform supporting high-availability web and mobile applications.
highlights: Architected Spring Boot microservices enabling independent deployment and horizontal scalability across distributed services.
Designed multi-database persistence with Spring Data JPA & MongoDB across MySQL, PostgreSQL, and MongoDB, supporting flexible data modeling per service.
Integrated Netflix OSS (Eureka, Zuul) to enable dynamic service discovery, load balancing, and centralized API gateway routing.
Configured Spring Cloud Config Server with fault-tolerance strategies, reducing configuration drift and improving system resilience.
Delivered production-ready RESTful APIs consumed by web and mobile clients within a cross-functional Agile team of 3–5 engineers.

work.name: Geor Workspace
work.summary: Built backend services for a multitenant SaaS platform with real-time communication and full-stack integration.
highlights: Built and maintained ASP.NET Core REST APIs serving web and mobile clients in a multitenant production environment.
Engineered a multitenant persistence layer with MongoDB and MySQL, enabling data isolation and scalability across multiple client organizations.
Delivered real-time chat between users and tenants by implementing WebSockets with ASP.NET Core SignalR, replacing polling and reducing server load.
Bridged backend and frontend by integrating ASP.NET Core services with an Angular application, owning the full request lifecycle.
Shipped features iteratively within a 3–5 person Agile team, contributing to sprint planning, code reviews, and daily stand-ups.

education.area: Bachelor's Degree in Computer Systems Engineering
Full Stack Development with MERN and SQL
